query: What are the core ramping and output level sustainance requirements for non-spinning reserves in ERCOT?
final_answer:
Under ERCOT’s current Non-Spinning Reserve (“Non-Spin”) rules, the core operating requirement is:

- **Bring the reserve online within 30 minutes** — for generation resources, that means being **synchronized and ramped to a specified output level within 30 minutes**; for controllable load resources, it means **ramping to an ERCOT-instructed consumption level within 30 minutes**. Source: ERCOT Ancillary Services Study (September 2024), Appendix 1 excerpting ERCOT Nodal Protocol Non-Spin requirements: https://www.ercot.com/files/docs/2024/10/07/ERCOT-Ancillary-Services-Study-Final-White-Paper.pdf

- **Sustain the instructed level for four consecutive hours** — ERCOT’s updated Non-Spin requirement is that the capacity be **“capable of being sustained for four consecutive hours.”** Source: ERCOT NPRR1096 issue page (“Require Sustained Two-Hour Capability for ECRS and Four-Hour Capability for Non-Spin”), approved 05/12/2022: https://www.ercot.com/mktrules/issues/NPRR1096

- **For generation resources specifically**, ERCOT’s study states they must be able to **run at the specified output level for at least four consecutive hours**. Source: ERCOT Ancillary Services Study (September 2024): https://www.ercot.com/files/docs/2024/10/07/ERCOT-Ancillary-Services-Study-Final-White-Paper.pdf

- **For controllable load resources**, ERCOT’s study states they must be able to **consume at the ERCOT-instructed level for at least four consecutive hours**. Source: ERCOT Ancillary Services Study (September 2024): https://www.ercot.com/files/docs/2024/10/07/ERCOT-Ancillary-Services-Study-Final-White-Paper.pdf

- **For non-controllable load resources**, ERCOT’s study states they must be able to **reduce consumption within 30 minutes** and **maintain that deployment until recalled**. Source: ERCOT Ancillary Services Study (September 2024): https://www.ercot.com/files/docs/2024/10/07/ERCOT-Ancillary-Services-Study-Final-White-Paper.pdf

So, in one line: **ERCOT Non-Spin must be able to ramp/interrupt within 30 minutes and then sustain the instructed output or load reduction for four consecutive hours (or until recalled for certain non-controllable load deployments).** Sources: ERCOT NPRR1096 issue page and ERCOT Ancillary Services Study linked above.
